orbitalTelescopeArray.name: Orbital Telescope Array
orbitalTelescopeArray.description: A constellation of imaging instruments placed in low orbit. Cheaper and faster than a full orbital platform, its primary value is signal: the array triangulates the transmission origin with an accuracy impossible from the ground. Requires a functioning Space Launch Centre.

hubbleSpaceTelescope.name: Orbital Imaging Platform
hubbleSpaceTelescope.description: A precision space telescope with spectroscopic and deep-field imaging capability. Above the atmosphere, the resolution improvements are transformative — anomalies in the outer solar system that appeared as statistical noise from the ground become geometric certainties. Requires Space Imaging technology and a Space Launch Centre.

cern.name: Particle Collider Complex
cern.description: A multi-kilometre underground ring accelerating particles to energies consistent with the mathematical framework in the signal's outer layers — not coincidentally. The outer structure of the transmission becomes legible at precision previously inaccessible. Expensive to construct and maintain, but the signal yield is substantial.
